channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: In the early going, Trump 2.0 approach on foreign policy is to talk loudly and carry a big stick
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump in his first week back in the White House has offered an early preview to his second-term foreign policy approach: Talk loudly and wield a big stick.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu hopes to meet Trump in Washington as soon as next week, US officials say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is hoping to meet with President Donald Trump in Washington as early as next week, according to two U.S. officials familiar with preliminary planning for the trip.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump pushes border crackdown at House Republican retreat as he caps first week in office
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump regaled House Republicans on Monday with a rhetorical highlight reel from his first week in office, and he urged them to support his immigration crackdown and border security proposals. “We have no apologies and we’re moving very fast," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, WILL WEISSERT and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: The Justice Department said Monday that it had fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal prosecutions of President Donald Trump, moving rapidly to pursue retribution against lawyers involved in the investigations and signaling an early willingness to take action favorable to the presi
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: New Trump orders on transgender troops, COVID and more expected on Hegseth's first day
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday he will sign a flurry of executive orders focused on the military, including to reinstate troops booted for refusing COVID-19 vaccines and remove “transgender ideology” from the force, just as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth began his first day on
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, LOLITA C. BALDOR and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In the early going, Trump 2.0 approach on foreign policy is to talk loudly and carry a big stick
item.description: Donald Trump in his return to the White House has already previewed his second term foreign policy approach: Talk loudly and wield a big stick.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: State AGs warn retail giant Costco for doubling down on 'discriminatory' DEI
item.description: State AGs in 19 states urge Costco to end discriminatory DEI policies, aligning with Trump’s executive order to enforce civil rights laws and investigate violators.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas Republicans in Congress propose bills to codify Trump executive orders
item.description: Ending birthright citizenship, increasing oil drilling, and leaving WHO are just a few bills the state’s Republicans pushed in Congress this month.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Katharine Wilson, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump Justice Department says it has fired employees involved in prosecutions of the president
item.description: The Trump Justice Department says it has fired more than a dozen employees who worked on criminal investigations into President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Three of Trump's most controversial Cabinet picks set for hearings
item.description: More hearings have been scheduled for Trump’s Cabinet nominees, marking a significant week ahead for some of his more controversial picks.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard's path through Intelligence Committee narrows ahead of key hearing
item.description: Republicans are cautioning that Tulsi Gabbard’s path to confirmation to lead the U.S. intelligence apparatus is narrowing as she seemingly has trouble winning over key GOP senators. Gabbard, along with Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Intelligence sharing by the US and its allies has saved lives. Trump could test those ties
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Russia moved closer to invading Ukraine nearly three years ago, the United States and its allies took the extraordinary step of declassifying and sharing intelligence to expose Moscow’s plans. Information flew across the Atlantic from U.S.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House says Colombia agrees to take deported migrants after Trump tariff showdown
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — The White House claimed victory in a showdown with Colombia over accepting flights of deported migrants from the U.S. on Sunday, hours after President Donald Trump threatened steep tariffs on imports and other sanctions on the longtime U.S. partner.
item.dc:creator: REGINA GARCIA CANO, ZEKE MILLER and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: DeSantis faces pushback by fellow Republicans on his call for an immigration session
item.description: Republican leaders in the state legislature say they support President Donald Trump's pledge to combat illegal immigration but want to deal with it and other issues identified by DeSantis on their schedule, not his.
item.dc:creator: Greg Allen, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he still wants to buy Greenland, suggests Canada could become a U.S. state
item.description: The newly inaugurated president held forth on multiple foreign policy issues on Saturday, from Greenland to Canada to the war between Israel and Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez, MPR News

item.title: Democrats are splintered on immigration and how to respond to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Donald Trump took office in 2017 with promises to crack down on immigration, he was met by widespread protests that filled churches, airports and union halls as Democratic lawmaker vowed to fight the new Republican president at every turn.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's whirlwind week back at the White House
item.description: President Trump stormed into the White House with a whirlwind of a first week, quickly making good on a number of campaign promises while embracing the trappings of the presidency.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's anti-DEI push puts top colleges in the crosshairs
item.description: Universities now find themselves at the frontlines of President Trump's war against diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) programs. An executive order this week instructed federal institutions to investigate DEI programs at schools with endowments of more than $1 billion endowments.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's border emergency declaration comes amid relative calm after years of major turmoil
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Long stretches of silence on a Border Patrol scanner are punctuated with updates on tracking a single migrant for hours. The radio traffic sounds like a throwback to earlier times, before the United States became the largest destination for asylum-seekers in 2017.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, GREGORY BULL and EUGENE GARCIA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Exclusive: White House in talks to have Oracle and U.S. investors take over TikTok
item.description: The aim is to place oversight control in the hands of American software company Oracle and other investors. Under federal law, TikTok must split apart from China, or face a nationwide ban.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: McConnell opposes Hegseth on final confirmation vote
item.description: His vote came as a surprise after he voted Thursday night to advance Hegseth’s nomination to a final vote.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's DOJ asks Supreme Court to freeze cases on student debt, environment
item.description: President Trump’s Justice Department on Friday asked the Supreme Court to freeze four pending cases implicating the environment or student debt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Bessent nomination to lead Treasury
item.description: The Senate on Saturday voted to advance Scott Bessent’s nomination to become the next secretary of the Treasury, putting him in line to be confirmed on Monday. Senators voted 67-23 to tee up final passage on Bessent’s nomination. The final vote will take place Monday evening at 5:30 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Florida schools will comply with ICE on campuses: Education department
item.description: Schools across America, including in Florida, are gearing up for the possibility of officials with Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) showing up to their campuses.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: St. Paul City Council President Mitra Jalali announces resignation, citing health concerns
item.description: After nearly seven years with the city, St. Paul council member and president Mitra Jalali has decided to step down. She says she is focusing on prioritizing her mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sam Stroozas, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he wants to overhaul FEMA as he travels to North Carolina and California
item.description: President Trump is taking the first trip of his term on Friday to North Carolina and California, visiting communities grappling with recovery from natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Asma Khalid and Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Trump orders release of JFK, RFK and MLK assassination records
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered the release of thousands of classified governmental documents about the 1963 assassination of President John F. Kennedy, which has fueled conspiracy theories for decades.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Top Minnesota attorney: Birthright citizenship suit against Trump just the beginning
item.description: A federal judge has temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship two days after Minnesota joined 21 other states in a lawsuit to challenge it. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison discussed his strategy.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Megan Burks, MPR News

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: U.S. District Judge John C. Coughenour ruled on Thursday in the case brought by the states of Washington, Arizona, Illinois and Oregon. The states argue that the 14th Amendment and Supreme Court case law have cemented birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump's new Justice Department leadership orders a freeze on civil rights cases
item.description: The new Justice Department leadership has put a freeze on civil rights litigation, and suggested it may reconsider police reform agreements negotiated by the Biden administration. That's according to two memos obtained Wednesday by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: NJ bill looks to eliminate hurdle for student service members
item.description: FLORHAM PARK, N.J. (PIX11) - From studying to serving: that was National Guard veteran Frank "Doc" Schupp's life in the late 2000s. He was called to deploy to Iraq at the end of his last semester of his junior year at Kean University, in the midst of prepping for finals.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Justice Department directs prosecutors to investigate local efforts to obstruct immigration enforcement
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Plan to reform 300 Nevada boards, commissions emerges
item.description: After Gov. Joe Lombardo took aim in his State of the State address at an ever-expanding number of boards and commissions that operate in Nevada with minimal oversight, a plan to reform the system is emerging.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What exactly is an executive order? Examining Trump's tool for quickly reshaping government
item.description: Donald Trump returned to the White House ready to immediately overhaul the government using the fastest tool he has — the executive order.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis: North Florida could see several inches of snow
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis warned of potential snowfall this week, amid the freezing temperatures impacting northern Florida.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why Biden pardoned Mark Milley ahead of Trump taking office
item.description: Former President Joe Biden's pre-emptive pardon for retired Gen. Mark Milley, the former chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, will give the retired military official a shield against any action that President Donald Trump might take against him amid their highly public feud.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, Fox 5
